prompt:
You need to set up automated code review for a project at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/review-project/'. The project uses GitHub and wants to enforce coding standards automatically.

Your setup task:

1) LINTING CONFIGURATION:
   - Set up ESLint with recommended rules
   - Configure Prettier for code formatting
   - Add TypeScript-specific rules if applicable
   - Create custom rules for project-specific standards

2) GIT HOOKS:
   - Set up pre-commit hooks to run linters
   - Configure commit message validation
   - Add branch naming convention checks
   - Set up pre-push hooks to run tests

3) CI/CD INTEGRATION:
   - Create GitHub Actions workflow for PR checks
   - Set up status checks (required before merge)
   - Configure code coverage reporting
   - Add automated security scanning

4) REVIEW TEMPLATES:
   - Create PR template with checklist
   - Set up issue templates for bug reports and features
   - Configure CODEOWNERS for automatic reviewer assignment
   - Add contribution guidelines

5) TESTING:
   - Run the automated checks against existing code
   - Fix any linting errors or formatting issues
   - Verify all hooks work correctly
   - Generate report showing code quality metrics

Output all configuration files to '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/review-config/' directory.
